read: Spot sitting just below the $730 zero-gamma ceiling with the $720 major put wall as the primary structural floor — expect a 720-730 range, with $715 the 0DTE backstop if 720 fails.

levels.why: Dominant structural put wall: 87k put OI, -444M GEX, and -40.6k charm creating massive dealer buy-back as put delta decays into close; 104% put vol/OI confirms this is a live battleground today, not standing inventory — highest-conviction floor in the data, but negative-gamma amplification keeps the bounce character mixed rather than clean.

Dual named ceiling — zero_gamma (730.24) AND vol_trigger (730.20) — where the regime flips to positive dealer gamma and amplification shuts off; 89k combined OI is the largest ATM mass, making it the mechanical cap for any bounce off 720, but price is already oscillating through this zone so tick entry is a grind not a snap.

Named 0DTE major put wall with the single largest charm reading in the entire dataset (-56.4k), surpassing even 720; the 0DTE charm pressure on dealers is at maximum with 118 mins left — if 720 fails in negative-gamma cascade, this level produces a faster, cleaner snap than 720 itself because dealer buy-back is mechanically forced and concentrated.

The single most actively contested strike today — 615% call vol/OI and 690% put vol/OI, the highest ratios in the data — with positive GEX and -7.4k charm reinforcing a ceiling; secondary resistance above 730 if a bounce overpowers the gamma-flip level, but 3.4 pts above spot in a downtrend keeps the probability low.

Primary named call wall, locked stable at 750 across all six today's wall-drift readings; 20+ pts from current spot makes it conditional on a full trend reversal — prior 67% score gutted by today's 740/735 breaks and expanded distance — but IF reached, concentrated OI creates a clean snap.
